observations.recommendation:
1.9. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Draw the appropriate adjusting entries to correct the misstatement of
depreciation taking into consideration the latest balances of the PPE
accounts affected;
b. Determine the cause of the differences between the schedules for
Water Supply Systems, Power Supply Systems, Buildings,
Slaughterhouses, School Buildings, Construction and Heavy
Equipment, and Medical Equipment PPE accounts and the
Accumulated Depreciation per Statement of Financial Position and
take appropriate actions to correct the same; and
c. Include in the Notes to the Financial Statements the estimated useful
lives used for each class of PPE and reasons for any deviations
therefrom for particular items of PPE, if warranted.

2.8. We recommended and Management agreed that for the property, plant and
equipment listed in Annex G and those not included in the list:
a. The Municipal Accountant submit to the Municipal Mayor a list of
PPEs in the possession and control of other government agencies
and non-government organizations for proper turnover;
b. The Municipal Mayor ensure that these PPEs in the possession and
control of other government agencies and non-government
organizations are properly turned over by execution of the necessary
contracts; and
c. The Municipal Accountant draw the appropriate accounting
entry/entries to drop from the books of accounts the PPEs in the
possession and control of other government agencies and non-
government organizations after proper turnover, taking into
consideration the latest balances of the accounts affected.

3.5. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw the
accounting entry/entries to reclassify the PPE items enumerated in Annex H to
their appropriate account classification, taking into consideration the latest
balances of the accounts affected.

4.8. We recommended and Management agreed that the following actions be
undertaken for the safeguarding of assets and immediate disposal of
unserviceable property items:
a. The Municipal Mayor ensure that junked or unserviceable property of the
Municipality are placed in a secured area to prevent theft and
further deterioration;
b. The Municipal GSO prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) covering all unserviceable property of
the municipality and submit the same to the Municipal Disposal
Committee;
c. The Disposal Committee conduct ocular inspection and recommend
the mode of disposal to be undertaken;
d. The Disposal Committee decide and recommend, as a collegial body,
one appraisal valuation for the property items for disposal;
e. The Municipal Mayor act on the recommendations of the Disposal
Committee and cause the actual disposal of the unserviceable
property items;
f. The Property Officer accomplish all pertinent portions of the IIRUP
after the disposal of unserviceable property items and submit the
accomplished form to the Municipal GSO who then report the same
to the Disposal Committee;
g. The Disposal Committee issue a certification on the disposal
addressed to the Municipal Accountant; and
h. The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to
drop the property items from the books of accounts and take up the
proceeds from the sale of property, if any.

5.9. We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Property and Accounting Units update their respective records and,
thereafter, compare both records and note the discrepancies;
b. Municipal Engineering, henceforth, furnish copies of inspection and
acceptance reports to the Property Office as their basis in recording
infrastructure assets;
c. Property Unit prepare and submit to the Inventory Committee, with
the latest RPCPPE, a list of PPE items which are recorded in the PPE
Ledger Cards (PPELCs) but not included in the RPCPPE as well as
PPE items which are included in the RPCPPE but not recorded in the
PPELCs;
d. Inventory Committee prepare and submit a Physical Inventory Plan
(PIP) to this Office at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled
start of inventory taking activities;
e. Municipal Mayor ensure that the Inventory Committee members be
temporarily relieved of all their regular duties until the conduct of the
physical inventory taking is completed in three months or less; and
f. Property and Accounting Units reconcile the inventory count per
RPCPPE with the property and accounting records within ten (10)
days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee,
investigate/verify any differences, and prepare the appropriate
adjustments on both records for reconciling items noted, and make
accountable persons liable for shortages or losses of PPE items, if
any.

6.4. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Look into prior records of the unidentified project with balance of
₱67,304.00 to determine its existence and accuracy;
b. Draw the appropriate journal entry/ies to correct the balance of the
Construction in Progress-Infrastruture Assets and related accounts;
and
c. Henceforth, ensure that amounts recorded as Construction in Progress
and Accounts Payable are based on the statement of work
accomplished and inspection reports and any adjustments thereto
upon actual payment be properly accounted for.

7.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the
elimination of intra-agency accounts in succeeding preparation of
consolidated financial statements to accurately reflect the agency’s
financial position.

8.5. We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer transfer to the
Trust Liabilities - Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund the
net amount of ₱96,347.89 that still remains in the General Fund;
b. Municipal Accountant revert the unexpended CY 2019 LDRRMF under
Trust Liabilities to General Fund and include as a source of fund to
finance other social services of the Municipality;
c. Municipal Accountant draw the necessary journal entries to correct
the subsidiary balances of the Trust Liabilities - Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund account; and
d. Municipal Accountant, henceforth, ensure that the LDRRMF be
properly accounted for in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002 to
reflect the correct balances of amounts to properly guide the
MDRRMC in its decision-making.

12.4. We recommended and Management agreed to continue the enforcement
of the settlement of audit disallowances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed that the
following steps, which are
taken from Module 2 of the
Local Road Asset
Management (LRAM)
Manual, be implemented:
a. The Municipal Mayor pass an executive order (EO) forming the Road Inventory Committee (RIC) and outlining their duties and responsibilities as defined in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
b. The Municipal Engineer organize the Physical Count Team and then conduct the physical count of the road networks.
c. The Physical Count Team identify the road network ID number and the road name on site vis-à-vis existing LGU records, if any, taking into consideration the provisions of the Local Road and Bridge Inventory and Condition Survey (LRBICS) Manual on the determination of road ID.
d. The Physical Count Team identify and provide a brief description of the components of the local roads into either road lot, pavement, drainage, slope protection structures, or other miscellaneous structures.
e. The Physical Count Team, together with the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant, ascertain the cost of each component using readily available data or specified replacement cost.
f. The Physical Count Team prepare and submit to this Office the Report of the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN), the format of which is found in Annex C of the COA Circular No. 2015-008, based on the information gathered from the foregoing steps.
g. The Municipal Accountant determine the useful life of each road asset.
h. The Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to record or adjust the correct balance of the Local Road Network account taking into consideration the details provided under the RPCLRN.
i. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO prepare the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.

observations.recommendation:

local roads and require the submission to the Municipal Accountant
of the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of
December 31, 2018.

1.7.1. the General Services Office or equivalent to prepare the Road
Map of the Municipality, prepare the Report on Local Road
Network and maintain the individual Local Road Network
Property Card;
1.7.2. the Municipal Engineering Office to provide the Municipal
Accounting Office and General Services Office with the
complete description, cost segregation by road component for
each road project; and
1.7.3. the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Report on Local
Road Network, Road Map, and the Report on the Physical
Count of the Road Network System with the Registry of Public
Infrastructure as of December 31, 2015 and prepare the
necessary Journal Entry Voucher to book-up the Road
Network; and maintain the individual Local Road Network
Ledger Cards.

book-up the remaining balance of ₱950,700.00 on ND No. 2015-001-
101(14) in the books of accounts to reflect correct amount of the
Receivable Disallowances/Charges account.

3.8. We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
3.8.1. require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts
with negative and dormant balances with the records of the
source agencies and effect the appropriate adjustments; and

refund of the unexpended balances of the fund transfers on
completed projects;

4.6. We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

and provincial share on RPT collections;
4.6.2. the Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents of
the negative & dormant balances; and reconcile with the
source agencies, where applicable; and to remit to the source
agency, or request for approval from the source agency for the
use of the same for other purposes; and
4.6.3. the Municipal Accountant to immediately reclassify the
collections on bid documents and interest on bank deposits for
the appropriate use of the Municipality.

review the original journal entry vouchers to record the Due to Other
Fund in the General and Trust Fund books of accounts; and analyze
and effect the necessary adjustments in order that the correct amount
of the reciprocal accounts will be reflected in the financial statements.

6.6. We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the

provided for in COA Circular No. 2012-02 to transfer the unspent

the Financial Statements of the Municipality and to establish proper
accountability on DRRMF.

review thoroughly the Financial Statements specially the Notes to

relative to the preparation and presentation of the FS by:

to cover structural defects/failures after the final acceptance of

from date of the Certificate of Completion and the Final

Municipal Treasurer to enforce immediate collection of the audit
disallowances which have become final and executory pursuant to

We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to revert the dormant accounts aged over three years that lack documents totalling P797,707.16, and henceforth, record only in the books those liabilities that meet the recognition criteria.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: a. require the Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents such as Memorandum of Agreement to determine the specific purpose of the fund transfers. This will assist the local officials in coming up with an informed decision whether to remit unexpended balances to the source agency, or request for approval from the source agency for the use of the same for other purposes; b. require the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate refund/remittance of the remaining PDAF to the National Treasury.

observations.reason: (Reiterated in Part II of this Report)

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that henceforth prepare and submit the report on RPCPPE within the required timeframe by considering the following: Segregate destroyed /unserviceable and obsolete items found and noted during the count and report them in the Inventory and Inspection of Unserviceable Property; Reassess the recorded unserviceable property and take the necessary actions for their immediate disposal in accordance with the rules on proper disposal; and Require the reconciliation of records between the Property Cards and Subsidiary ledger cards to provide check and balance of accounting and property records.
observations.managementAction: a. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct a thorough physical inventory taking of all property owned by the municipality and prepare the RPCPPE. b. Segregate destroyed /unserviceable and obsolete items found and noted during the count and report them in the Inventory and Inspection of Unserviceable Property; c. Reassess the recorded unserviceable property and take the necessary actions for their immediate disposal in accordance with the rules on proper disposal; d. Require the reconciliation of records between the Property Cards and Subsidiary ledger cards to provide check and balance of accounting and property records.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management strengthen its internal control on the submission, recording and deposit of collections by: requiring the Municipal Treasurer to establish proper cut-off in the reporting of collections, particularly the daily accomplishment of the Report of Collections and Deposits by the Revenue Collection Clerks; requiring the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all RCDs turned-over by the RCCs are all recorded in the Summary of RCDs, and deposited on the date it was received; and requiring the Municipal Treasurer to supervise and monitor the RCCs on the reporting of issued accountable forms and submission of RCDs.
observations.managementAction: a. The Municipal Treasurer verbally required the RCCs to submit their collection and accomplish the RCDs daily. b. The Municipal Treasurer verbally instructed the Liquidating Officer to report all RCDs. c. The Municipal Treasurer reminded her Revenue Collection Clerks. d. No accountable officer incurred a shortage.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: require the Municipal Treasurer to record transactions daily, foot and close the books at the end of each month; and require the Municipal Treasurer to initiate reconciliation of the cashbook balances with the General Ledgers of the Municipal Accountant, at least on a quarterly basis, in order that the correct valuation of cash balances are reflected in both books.
observations.managementAction: a. The Municipal Treasurer recorded transactions daily, foot and close the books at the end of each month; and b. The Municipal Treasurer requested copies of journal entry on items identified by the Accounting Office and made the necessary adjustments, if needed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the MDRRMC henceforth, monitor and fast track the implementation of all programmed and funded projects under the LDRRMF for the current year.
observations.managementAction: a. The LDRRMP for CY 2018 was prepared promptly and the specific DRRM PPAs were integrated in the Annual Appropriation totaling P5,000,250.00. b. The MLGU identified the reasons for the late implementation as one of the causes of delay. c. The MLGU monitored and partially complied with the recommendation; one (1) prior year project remained on-going as of 12/31/18. For CY 2018, eight (8) road rehabilitation projects allocated from the CY 2018 QRF upon the declaration of a state of calamity in June 2018 were not immediately implemented to reduce the risk brought about by the typhoon. Instead, the project commenced only on December 12, 2018 resulting in uncompleted projects as of 12/21/18.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection of delinquent real property taxes by notifying delinquent tax payers through posting of Notices of Delinquency as required in Section 254 of Republic Act No. 7160; and conduct information and education campaign (IEC) on real property tax payment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent Notices to delinquent tax payers and posted the Lists at the different barangay halls and IECs were also conducted in some barangays. The Municipality was able to collect P127,294.10 on Real Property Revenue, Fines and Penalties-RPT totaling P28,879.71.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management to: require the offices of the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to conduct regular monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that infrastructure projects are accomplished in accordance with the terms of the contract; and require the MPDC to monitor and evaluate regularly the implementation of the different development programs, projects and activities in the LGU, in accordance with the approved development plan and submit periodic reports to the Local Chief Executive as his basis in taking immediate appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a final notice of slippage to seven (7) contractors prompting the contractors to complete the projects. The Municipal Engineer has been monitoring the implementation of projects. The MPDC monitored and supervised the implementation and submitted periodic reports. The MLGU has been deducting the computed liquidated damages from contractors' claims as appropriate.

observations.managementAction: a. The Municipal Treasurer issued follow-up letters to the contractors prompting compliance. b. The Municipal Engineer has been instructed to monitor the compliance of contractors to the plans and specifications. d. No action taken on the submission of report on unused and/or reusable form lumbers as of reporting.

observations.managementAction: Management encouraged all barangays to put up their own Materials Recovery Facilities (MRFs).

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: look into the source documents such as Memorandum of Agreements or equivalent documents to determine the specific purpose of the fund transfers. This will assist the local officials in coming up with an informed decision whether to remit unexpended balances to the source agency, or request for approval from the source agency for the use of the same for other purposes; reconcile the balances with the records of the source agencies; review the individual subsidiary ledgers of the accounts with negative balances and effect the appropriate adjustments for the deficiencies noted; and require the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate refund of the unexpended balances of fund transfers on completed projects covered with Memorandum of Agreements or equivalent.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant said that the unexpended balance of ₱77,377.00 would not be refunded to SN Aboitiz as they were not required to. Instead, she would reclassify the fund to the subsidy account. For the negative balances, she assured that these were not a result of overpayment, instead these were accounting errors which she has to review and effect the necessary adjustments. As for the other recommendations, both the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer agreed to comply.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the barangay and provincial share on RPT collections; the Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents such as Memorandum of Agreements or equivalent documents to determine the reasons on the negative balances and the unexpended dormant balances and where applicable to remit to the source agency, or request for approval from the source agency for the use of the same for other purposes; the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances with the records of the source agencies; and the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate reclassification of the collections on bid documents and interest on bank deposits for the appropriate use of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer explained that she remits the barangay RPT shares based on accumulated amount because the meager shares are impractical to be remitted on a quarterly basis. The Municipal Accountant added that she will have to review first the details of those RPT shares recorded ten (10) years back in the Trust Fund totaling P347,006.09 before it will be remitted to the barangays concerned. Further, the Municipal Accountant said that the MLGU will have to account only the amount of P2,500,000.00 representing the CY 2017 Gotad prize to the Provincial Government of Ifugao since all the other funds received from the PLGU were erroneously recorded in the books of the MLGU as payables instead of a subsidy account, which the PLGU used to record them.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed that the: Municipal Accountant prepare and certify the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF, and the MDRRMO to submit the same to the Audit Team through the LDRRMC and LDC on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and certify the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF; and b. the MDRRMO to submit the same to the Audit Team through the LDRRMC and LDC on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to review thoroughly the Financial Statements especially the Notes to Financial Statements to comply with the PPSAS and COA Circulars relative to the preparation and presentation of the FS by providing adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements and presenting the breakdown and figures of all accounts being cross-referenced in the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce immediate collection of the audit disallowances which have become final and executory pursuant to Section 7.1.3 and the COA Order of Execution.

The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that as of January 2019, they have discontinued collecting from the municipal employees the disallowed PEI because they have sent a letter to the COA Regional Director requesting for reconsideration on the disallowed PEI in February 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management to continue the LGU’s good practices on solid waste management for the favorable promotion of sanitation and health in the locality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality substantially complied with the criteria on environmental management and was commended. Management to continue good practices.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: a. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to accomplish their RCDs and submit their collections to the Liquidating Officer at the end of each business day or as they occur, in accordance with accounting rules and regulations; b. instruct the Liquidating Officer to refrain from holding RCDs to be reported on a later date. Instead, she has to summarize all RCDs turned over to her by the RCCs and turn-over intact to the Municipal Treasurer; c. require the Liquidating Officer to comply with the procedures for the receipt and verification of collections turned over to him. He shall also accomplish the RCD in four copies to summarize the collections turned over to him by the collectors/tellers as well as his own collections; d. require the Liquidating Officer to turn over intact the cash collections to the Treasurer/Cashier together with the originals and two copies of the RCDs of collectors/tellers and the duplicate of the official receipts issued; and e. require the Treasurer/Cashier to deposit intact all his collections as well as all collections turned over to him by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day; and strictly impose administrative sanctions to erring Accountable Officers, when necessary.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: require the Revenue Collecting Officers to prepare their RAAF at the end of each month and the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate the reports to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer did not submit a report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management adopt the internal control measures in its payroll systems to promote good governance at the local level by requiring the Municipal Treasurer to avail of the bank’s ATM for LGU employees’ salaries and wages.
observations.managementAction: Management and employees agreed not to avail of LBP ATM on the payment of salaries. Employees preferred to be paid in cash.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: a. avoid lump-sum appropriation in the annual/supplemental budget; b. require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Council to submit a revised LDRRMFIP on the approved LDRRMF and require the Municipal Budget Officer to henceforth release allotment only based on approved LDRRMFIP incorporated in the local development fund; and c. fast tract the implementation of the programs and activities identified in the LDRRMIP to enhance both the personnel and equipment, in managing disaster risk activities of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMC prepared the LDRRMFIP on May 23, 2017, instead of from May to June 2016.

observations.recommendation: 1.8. We have recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant to revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund the dormant accounts aged over three years that lack documents totaling P797,707.16, for fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements and henceforth, record only in the books those liabilities that meet the recognition criteria.

2.7. We have recommended and Management agreed to:

This will assist the local officials in coming up with an informed decision whether to remit unexpended balances to the source agency, request for approval from the source agency the use of the same for other purposes, or to revert the same to the General Fund for proper appropriation; and
2.7.2. require the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate refund/remittance of the remaining PDAF to the National Treasury.

We have recommended and management agreed to advise the Local School Board to ensure that the Special Education Fund be budgeted, implemented and utilized in accordance with Item 3.0 of DepEd/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017, which implements RA No. 7160, to promote quality education in every public school.

13.8. We have recommended and Management agreed to:
13.8.1. clearly provide qualitative and quantitative data that would support the gender issues identified in the GPB, especially if the PPA is intended to be a GAD-focused activity taking 100% of its allocation from GAD;

13.8.3. if and when necessary, conduct a training program on the HGDG for the incoming year and seek assistance of a gender analysis expert in the use of and/administration of the HGDG.

We have recommended that Management consider the following measures: a. require the concerned officials/employees to immediately settle their accounts through submission of liquidation documents and to refund any excess amount, otherwise, write-off withholding of salary and/or other sanctions shall be imposed upon them in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; b. suspend the granting of cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer and other officials/employees until their previous cash advance have been liquidated, to prevent accumulation of their unliquidated cash advances; c. comply with the submission of the required supporting documents for cash advances; d. limit the grant of cash advances for operating expenses that could not be paid directly by check to suppliers; and e. strictly adhere to laws, rules, and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002, to ensure efficient and effective management of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Management: a. require the concerned officials/ employees to immediately settle their accounts through submission of liquidation documents and to refund any excess amount, otherwise, write-off withholding of salary and/or other sanctions shall be imposed upon them in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; b. suspend the granting of cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer and other officials/employees until their previous cash advance have been liquidated, to prevent accumulation of their unliquidated cash advances; c. comply with the submission of the required supporting documents for cash advances; d. limit the grant of cash advances for operating expenses that could not be paid directly by check to suppliers;; and e. strictly adhere to laws, rules, and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002, to ensure efficient and effective management of government funds.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the General Fund books the said medical equipment by preparing adjusting entries to the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the recommendation by preparing adjusting entries to recognize the medical equipment in the General Fund books.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the accounts and require the recipient barangays to submit the required reports within ten (10) days after the end of the month/end of the agreed period for the program, in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation and reclassified the accounts and required the recipient barangays to submit the required reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management to: a. require the Municipal Accountant to strictly the guidelines provided under LBC No. 103 dated May 15, 2013, on the grant of Representation and Transportation Allowances to all LGU officials entitled thereto; b. Through the Vice Mayor, as the presiding chair of the SB, instigate the adoption/inclusion of rules in the IRP on the work schedules/performance standards of all members, which shall be used in the determination of percentage of RATA to be granted hereafter, in accordance with Item 7.5 of the above LBC; c. henceforth, support RATA payrolls of the SB with the required documentary requirements; and d. cause them to observe the rules on leave of absences.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant implemented strictly the recommendation. The SB has adopted their internal rules on the conduct of session and performance standards. The payrolls of the SB with required documentary requirements were supported and application for leave of absences were filed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: a. require the officials/employees concerned to immediately submit the contracts’ supporting documents as identified in Annex A to enable the audit team to complete auditorial review; b. require all winning bidders to post performance security before the execution of the contract agreement in the form and in the amount stipulated in the Instructions to Bidders and in compliance with the 2016 IRR of RA 9184; and c. strictly comply with the submission of the contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof to the auditor concerned within five days after its execution, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 and the 2016 IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal officials/ employees concerned immediately submitted the contracts’ supporting documents and contractors posted performance security before the execution of the contract agreement. Contracts and supporting documents were submitted to the auditor within five days after execution.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: a. require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the 2016 documentary requirements stipulated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 pertaining to the payment of salaries of employees on job orders; and b. require the Human Resource Officer to submit all Contracts of Service/Job Orders with the Statement of duties and responsibilities of job orders for CY 2016 and onwards to the Auditor concerned, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant complied with the recommendation and the Human Resource Officer compiled and submitted Job Orders and Statement of duties and responsibilities.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: require the LDRRM Council to submit a revised LDRRMFIP on the approved LDRRMF and require the Municipal Budget Officer to henceforth release allotment only based on approved LDRRMFIP incorporated in the local development fund.
observations.managementAction: Management partly implemented the recommendation and the Allotment was released based on the approved LDRRMF and budget.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to: a) clearly provide qualitative and quantitative data that would support the gender issues identified in the GPB, especially if the PPA is intended to be a GAD-focused activity taking 100% of its allocation from GAD; b) administer the HGDG test on the GAD PPAs in the preparation of GPB and AR, as basis for determining the actual expenditure that can be attributed to the GAD Budget; and c) if and when necessary, conduct a training program on the HGDG for the incoming year and seek assistance of a gender analysis expert in the use of and/administration of the HGDG.
observations.managementAction: Partly complied with: Gender Sensitivity Training and in-house training conducted to the GAD Focal Point System and barangay officials; preparation of GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report were done.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and agreed to: a) follow up the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan; b) require the establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities in the 16 barangays; and c) strictly comply with the other provisions of the RA No. 9003 especially on open dump prohibition against the maintenance of open dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: Followed up the approval of the 10-year ESWMP. The Montabiong open dump was closed. Materials Recovery Facilities were established in nearby barangays.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to continue enforcing the settlement of disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to continue enforcement and collection actions.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to: a) require the contractor on the National Child Development Center to submit a Statement of Work Accomplishment or remedial measures done on the deficiencies noted; otherwise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deduct the corresponding amount of deficiencies from his money claim; b) require personnel of the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct regular monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that infrastructure projects are accomplished in accordance with the terms of the contract; c) ensure the immediate completion of all on-going projects which already have incurred delays; and d) direct the Municipal Engineer to determine the percentage of completion upon expiry of the contract duration of the said projects, as basis for the computation of the liquidated damages which will be collected from the contractors.
observations.managementAction: Management required the contractors to rectify deficiencies and awaited responses; some projects were appealed for consideration and change orders were requested. Final Notice of Slippage was sent to contractors of several projects; one contract was terminated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management observe the guidelines on designation in the civil service by considering the following: a) designate personnel with appropriate qualifications for the position they are designated for a maximum of one year; b) the Human Resource Officer to adopt innovative ways to attract applicants for the vacant positions; and c) fill up the vacant positions with the most qualified personnel through a wider reach of publication and posting process.
observations.managementAction: The positions of Population Officer and the Municipal Health Officer were filled; Human Resource Officer conducted measures to attract qualified applicants.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain her cashbooks properly and regularly and reconcile her balances with the accounting records at least quarterly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer maintained her cashbooks properly and reconciled her balances with the accounting records quarterly.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management limit the hiring of such personnel through Job Orders only for approved local projects authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan, pursuant to Section 77 of RA 7160 and hire casual appointments pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 40, series of 1998, subject to limitation.
observations.managementAction: The hiring of JOs was not based solely on approved local projects; JOs continued to be engaged for essential services. Wages were charged to General Services account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to advise the Local School Board to ensure that the Special Education Fund be budgeted, implemented and utilized in accordance with Item 3.0 of DepEd/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017, which implements RA No. 7160, to promote quality education in every public school.
observations.managementAction: The fund was partially utilized for SEF purposes; management advised the Local School Board to comply with DepEd/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s.2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management cause the refund of the amount of excess PEI granted to officials and employees and submit request for reconsideration where appropriate.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted request for reconsideration and partially collected the disallowance. Collection actions realized amounts from officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: identify properly all government vehicles by marking "FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY" on each side of the vehicle and underneath the name of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Not all vehicles were marked "FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY" on each side; AOM No. 2018-07 is dated March 20, 2018 was issued.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the contractors to rectify their deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: The contractors to address the above defects accordingly or refund the computed deficiencies. The project on Rehabilitation of Cuta-Bahawit Nagawa Road was rectified; the Renovation/Improvement of the SB hall deficiencies was not rectified.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to require the Revenue Collecting Officers to prepare their RAAF at the end of each month and the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate the reports to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: Management did not comply with the recommendation. (Reiterated in Part II of this Report)

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to:
a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the RCCs to strictly accomplish their
RCDs and submit their collections to the Liquidating Officer at the end of each
business day or as they occur, in accordance with accounting rules and
regulations;
b) instruct the Liquidating Officer to refrain from holding RCDs to be reported on
27
a later date. Instead, she has to summarize all RCDs turned over to her by the
RCCs and turn-over intact to the Municipal Treasurer;
c) require the Liquidating Officer to strictly check the statement of accountable
forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the new ending
balances on hand and make physical count of the accountable forms
remaining in the custody of the RCCs; and
d) impose administrative sanctions to erring Accountable Officers, when
necessary.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the Revenue Collecting
Officers to prepare their RAAF at the end of each month and the Municipal
Treasurer to consolidate the reports to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not
later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

We have recommended and Management agreed to consider the following measures:
a) require concerned officials/employees with unliquidated cash advances to
immediately settle their accounts through submission of liquidation documents
and to refund any excess amount, otherwise, withholding of salary and/or other
sanctions shall be imposed upon them in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-
002;
b) suspend the granting of cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer and other
officials/employees until their previous cash advance have been liquidated, to
prevent accumulation of their unliquidated cash advances;
c) limit the grant of cash advances for operating expenses which could not be paid
directly by check to suppliers;
d) comply with the submission of the required supporting documents for cash
advances; and
e) strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002, to ensure
30
efficient and effective management of government funds.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize in the
General Fund books the said medical equipment by preparing adjusting entries to
book the same.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the
accounts and require the recipient barangays to submit the required reports within
ten (10) days after the end of each month/end of the agreed period for the
program, in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and-- 2015-009.

We have recommended and management agreed to:
a) require the Municipal Accountant to implement strictly the guidelines
provided under LBC No. 103 dated May 15, 2013, on the grant of
Representation and Transportation Allowances to all LGU officials entitled
thereto;
b) through the Vice Mayor, as the presiding chair of the SB, instigate the
adoption/inclusion of rules in the IRP on the work schedules/performance
standards of all members, which shall be used in the determination of
percentage of RATA to be granted hereafter, in accordance with Item 7.5 of
38
the above LBC;
c) henceforth, support RATA payrolls of the SB with required documentary
requirements; and
d) advise them to observe the rules on leave of absences.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
a. instruct the officials/employees concerned to submit immediately the
contracts’ supporting documents as identified in Annex E to enable the audit
team to complete auditorial review;
b. require all winning bidders to post performance security before the execution
of the contract agreement, in the form and in the amount stipulated in the
Instructions to Bidders and in compliance with the 2016 IRR of RA 9184: and
c. strictly comply with the submission of contracts and each of all the documents
forming part thereof to the auditor concerned within five days after its
execution, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 and the 2016 IRR
of RA 9184.

We have recommended that management adopt internal control measures in its
payroll systems to promote good governance at the local level by requiring the
Municipal Treasurer to avail of the bank’s ATM for LGU employees’ salaries
and wages.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
a) require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly
comply with the documentary requirements stipulated under COA Circular
42
No. 2012-001 pertaining to the payment of salaries of employees on job
orders; and
b) require the Human Management Resource Officer to submit all Contracts of
Service/Job Orders with the Statement of Duties and Responsibilities of Job
Orders for CY 2016 and onwards to the Audit Team, in compliance with
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
a) avoid lump-sum appropriation in the annual/supplemental budget;
b) require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to
observe the timeline of preparing/submitting the LDRRMFIP to the Local
Development Council so that PPAs for LDRRM be integrated in the Annual
Investment Plan and eventually the Annual Budget; and
c) fast tract the implementation of the programs and activities identified in the
LDRRMIP to enhance both the personnel and equipment, in managing
disaster risk activities of the municipality.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the LDRRM Council to
submit a revised LDFFMFIP on the use of prior years’ LDRRMF and require the
Municipal Budget Officer to henceforth release allotment only based on approved
LDRRMFIP incorporated in the local development fund.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require all elected officials and
heads of offices to undergo capacity-building programs on GAD. For economy,
invite competent speakers and conduct in-house training to maximize the
participation of the focal point system, elected officials, heads of offices,
employees and other stakeholders, i.e. Barangay officials, to harmonize GAD-
related activities in the ensuing year. Such capacity building programs on GAD
shall include, but not limited to: gender sensitivity training, gender-responsive
planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools such as the
Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework, Gender Responsive LGU Ka Ba
Self-Assessment Toll, Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines and
others.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
a) follow up the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan;
b) require the establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities in the 16 barangays:
and
c) strictly comply with the other provisions of RA No. 9003 especially on
prohibition against the maintenance of open dumpsite.

We have recommended and management agreed to continue enforcing the
settlement of disallowances.
We have recommended and Management agreed to maintain her cashbooks properly and reconcile her accounting records at least quarterly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer partially complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to strictly secure the safe for accountable forms and monitor the issuance thereof to avoid unaccounted accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the value of the donated ambulance and the constructed National Child Development Center in the LGUs books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the accounts under JEV#2015-04-001094 and JEV #2016-01-000208.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and the Municipal Budget Officer to provide in the Annual Investment Program the specific details of projects to be undertaken and funded out of the share from national wealth, for the ensuing years.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to use the Annual Investment Program as basis for the preparation of the Annual and Supplemental Budgets pursuant to Section 305 (i), R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to comply with Section 336 of RA No. 7160 on the 'Use of Appropriated Funds and Savings' by following the requirement of the enacting municipal ordinance in augmenting items in the approved annual budget.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to comply with Section 322 of RA No. 7160 on the reversion of unexpended balances of appropriations, and the enactment of appropriation ordinance to avoid questionable transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to cease from implementing programs that are within the realm of the executive office and for the ensuing years, their appropriation for MOOE should be limited to the regular maintenance and operating expenses of the office.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management facilitate the closure of the open dump to ensure compliance with RA No. 9003 and to avoid being filed charges by the Environmental Bureau.
observations.managementAction: Management completed the closure of the Montabiong dump site previously questioned by the Environmental Bureau however, management opened another 300 sq.m. dump area in Montabiong.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management limit the hiring of such personnel through contracts of service and only for approved local projects authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan, pursuant to Section 77 of RA 7160 and hire personnel through appointments pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 40, series of 1998, for essential and necessary services, subject to PS limitation.
observations.managementAction: Management partially complied with the recommendation. The number of personnel was reduced and their wages was decreased by P3,406,541.50.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with the pertinent rules of COA Circular No. 2012-02 that only those expenditure items that support disaster management activities will be included in the LDRRMIP.
observations.managementAction: Management did not push through the proposed projects that do not support disaster management.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management fast tract the implementation of the programs and activities identified in the LDRRMIP to enhance both the personnel involved in managing the disaster related activities, and the DRRM equipment of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management purchased some lifesaving and rescue equipment and conducted trainings utilizing prior years’ appropriation however, it did not implement the PPAs for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to require the Local School Board to comply strictly with the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 to ensure that the Special Education Fund be budgeted, and exclusively and fully utilized for the purpose for which it was created.
observations.managementAction: Management partially complied with the recommendation because of the late preparation of the SEF AIP. The Four expenditure items of the fund were not utilized totaling P110,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC to: strictly comply with RA No. 9184 and the GPPB rules and regulations and make certain that the requisite terms and conditions are completely embodied in the ITB/Request for Quotation; ensure that all necessary information are provided in the Request for Quotation/Invitation to Bid to enable effective evaluation of prices and to warrant that each award of contract shall be made to the supplier who not only has the lowest offer but also complies with the specifications and other terms and conditions stated therein.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to continue enforcing the settlement of disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management have partially collected through deduction from salaries/other claims of the payees and other disallowances were on appeal.
observations.reason: The amount of disallowance was reduced by P227,688.87.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal GAD Focal Point agreed to present in the Annual Plan and Budget the specific details PPAs to be funded to allow realistic estimation of pertinent cost by the expenditure.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management rescind the contract on the Installation of Streetlights along the National Road at Poblacion, Lagawe and blacklist the bidder from participating from any procurement bid in the LGU. Likewise, we have recommended Management to require the Municipal Engineer to determine the percentage of accomplishment at the time of contract expiry as basis in the computation of liquidated damages for the unperformed portion of the project, which shall be paid by the contractor.
observations.managementAction: Management issued letter of termination of contract to the contractor which he accepted. The contract was rescinded.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management maximize the use of the fund to ensure that the needs of the constituents are met by: lowering the cost of electricity either through subsidy and non-subsidy scheme or a combination of both; providing alternative power sources like affordable generators or solar panel lights for the residents from areas that cannot be energized directly from the grid; and implementing the development and livelihood programs that could lead to economic and social results in for the long term benefits for the people.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation. The 80% portion of the fund was utilized for the payment of electricity expenses of the municipality. However, it did not provide the streetlights. The collection on National Wealth decreased by P2,624,969.38.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management consider the following measures: to strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations in the utilization of funds to ensure economy and efficiency in the management of government funds; limit the conduct of meetings/sessions within the immediate jurisdiction of the LGU, instead of island regions.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management cause the refund of the excess PEI granted on CY 2014 PEI and submit request for reconsideration.
observations.managementAction: Management collected amounts relating to PEI; some actions taken and amounts collected for disallowed PEI and related items.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended identify properly all government vehicles by marking “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” on each side of the vehicle and underneath is the name of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: One vehicle was marked.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management continue enforcing the settlement of disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management collected the amount of P227,688.87 for disallowed PEI for CY 2013.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management: a) instruct the implementing offices to fast tract the implementation of programs/projects/activities to ensure that the needs of the constituents are met and the municipality is prepared in times of disaster.
observations.managementAction: Management partially complied with the recommendation. The 5 projects for CY 2015 remained unfinished as of December 2016 and 37 fully funded projects for CY 2016 were also on-going as of yearend.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management cause the full settlement/liquidation, soonest, of past due cash advances by issuing demand letters to concerned accountable officers of the LGU and NGAs including those who are separated from the service or have transferred to other agencies.
observations.managementAction: Management partially complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the contractors to rectify their deficiencies accordingly or refund the computed deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: Management partially implemented. The project on Rehabilitation of Cutabahawit Nagawa Road was rectified but the deficiencies on the Renovation/Improvement of the SB hall was not rectified.
